app.auth.verifyingBody: Authenticating with the saved access key...
app.auth.inputRequired: Please enter the admin access key
app.auth.securityItem1: The admin console uses ADMIN_ACCESS_KEY; it falls back to PROXY_ACCESS_KEY if unset.
app.auth.securityItem2: The key is stored locally and will be reused for automatic sign-in.
app.auth.securityItem3: Do not share your access key with others.
app.auth.securityItem4: Change the server config immediately if you suspect a leak.
app.auth.securityItem5: After {attempts} failed attempts, access is locked for 5 minutes.
cockpit.overrideActive: Custom channel order is active. Auto-reset in {time}
cockpit.overrideActivePerpetual: Custom channel order active (will not expire until manually restored)
cockpit.empty: No active flights. Conversations will appear on radar when requests pass through the gateway.
cockpit.noMatches: No conversations match the current filter.
cockpit.feedbackPlaceholder: Feedback for AI, e.g. prefer the low-latency channel for this subagent
channels.empty.title: No channels yet
channels.empty.description: No API channel is configured yet. Add your first channel to start using the proxy service.
capability.title: Capability test - {channel}
capability.noModelsFound: No models found. Add models manually below.
capability.noApiKeyError: This channel has no API key configured and cannot run capability tests.
orchestration.title: Channel orchestration
orchestration.dragHint: Drag to change priority, saved automatically
orchestration.enableFromPool: Enable channels from the standby pool below
orchestration.appendToFailover: Once enabled, it will be appended to the end of the failover sequence
orchestration.promotionSuccess: Channel {name} is now top priority and will be preferred for the next 5 minutes
orchestration.resumeSuccessWithKeys: Channel resumed, metrics reset, and {count} blacklisted key(s) restored
orchestration.deleteActiveGuard: Cannot delete: keep at least one active channel in the failover sequence
orchestration.cacheWriteHighHint: The cache write ratio is high in the last 15 minutes, which may indicate suboptimal cache-hit configuration.
addChannel.quickInputHint: Paste configuration text containing Base URL and API Key, the system will parse automatically
addChannel.quickInputPlaceholder:
Paste config snippet to auto-detect Base URL and API Key (multi-line supported)
https://api.example.com
https://api-backup.example.com
sk-ant-api03-xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
sk-ant-api03-yyyyyyyyyyyyyyyy
addChannel.enterApiKey: Enter at least one API key
copilotOAuth.description: Use GitHub OAuth to authorize Copilot. After authorization, the GitHub OAuth token is added as a key for this channel.
addChannel.streamTimeoutDescription: Configure timeout parameters for streaming responses
addChannel.streamFirstByteTimeoutHint: Maximum time to wait for the first response content
addChannel.streamChunkIntervalTimeoutHin: Maximum interval between two data chunks
addChannel.streamOverallTimeoutHint: Maximum duration for the entire streaming response
addChannel.baseUrlPlaceholder:
One URL per line, supports multiple Base URLs
Example:
https://api.openai.com/v1
https://api2.openai.com/v1
addChannel.modelHint: Configure source model to target model mapping here.
addChannel.modelHintTip: Click a target model field to load supported upstream models. Detection status for each API key appears in the key list.
addChannel.sourceModelPlaceholder: Select or enter a source model
addChannel.reasoningParamStyleHint: Choose whether OpenAI-style upstream requests use reasoning.effort or reasoning_effort.
addChannel.supportedModelsPlaceholder: Press Enter after each rule, for example gpt-4*, *image*, or !*image*
addChannel.supportedModelsHint: Matches the original model requested by the client before model redirect is applied. Leave empty to allow all models. Supported forms: exact, prefix*, *suffix, *contains*, and exclusion with !.
addChannel.supportedModelsInvalidPattern: Invalid rule ignored. Only exact, prefix*, *suffix, *contains*, and ! exclusion are allowed.
addChannel.defaultContextWindowHint: Used when the actual model is not covered by the built-in registry or JSON overrides. Empty uses the unknown-model policy.
addChannel.defaultMaxOutputHint: Only filters requests that explicitly set an output limit.
addChannel.modelCapabilitiesJsonHint: Keys use the actual model name after model redirect and may use wildcards. Empty object means no built-in override.
addChannel.modelCapabilitiesJsonInvalid: Enter a valid JSON object. Values may only include contextWindowTokens, maxOutputTokens, thinkingMode, and reasoningEfforts.
addChannel.allowUnknownContextLabel: Allow unknown models for large context
addChannel.allowUnknownContextHint: By default, unknown-capability models are candidates only up to 200K tokens. Enable this to allow larger requests.
addChannel.apiKeyRequired: At least one key is required
addChannel.apiKeyLoadBalance: Multiple keys can be added as failover candidates
addChannel.skipTlsHint: Enable temporarily only for self-signed certificates or domain mismatch. Keep disabled in production.
addChannel.lowQualityHint: When enabled, token usage is force-estimated locally and the local value is used if the deviation exceeds 5%.
addChannel.autoBlacklistBalanceHint: Automatically move the key to the blacklist when the upstream reports insufficient balance.
addChannel.codexNativeToolPassthroughHin: Convert Codex native tools (apply_patch, namespace, etc.) to OpenAI function format in passthrough mode, so upstream models can invoke them.
addChannel.codexToolCompatHint: Enable Codex CLI compatibility: strip client-only tools for Responses passthrough upstreams, or convert them to function proxy tools for Chat/Claude/Gemini upstreams.
addChannel.stripImageGenerationToolHint: Remove image_generation from the tools array to avoid upstream permission errors when image generation is not enabled.
addChannel.convertImageUrlToB64JsonHint: When the client requests response_format=b64_json but the upstream only returns a URL, download the image and convert it to b64_json.
addChannel.normalizeMetadataUserIdHint: Auto-convert JSON object user_id to a flat string for upstream compatibility.
addChannel.stripBillingHeaderHint: Remove cch= billing parameters from system text blocks before forwarding, only for the current Messages channel.
addChannel.stripEmptyTextBlocksHint: Remove bare empty text content blocks before forwarding, for Claude-protocol upstreams that strictly reject Claude Code tool-use placeholders.
addChannel.normalizeSystemRoleToTopLevel: For Opus 4.8 / Fable 5 / similar new clients that emit system as a message role: extract them back to the top-level system field before forwarding, for legacy Claude upstreams that only accept user/assistant roles.
addChannel.normalizeNonstandardChatRoles: Converts non-standard roles like developer to user before forwarding upstream. Recommended for domestic Chinese models that lack non-standard role support.
addChannel.stripCodexClientToolsHint: Enable only for legacy Responses upstreams that reject Codex CLI 0.130+ tool schemas.
addChannel.injectDummyThoughtSignatureHi: Injects a dummy signature into functionCall for third-party APIs that require this field. Disable it for the official API.
addChannel.stripThoughtSignatureHint: Removes thought_signature from functionCall for older Gemini APIs that do not support it.
addChannel.noVisionHint: When enabled, requests with images will skip this channel and failover to the next
addChannel.visionDisabled: This model does not support image input
addChannel.visionFallbackPlaceholder: Model to use when request contains images
addChannel.visionFallbackHint: When a request contains images and the target model does not support vision, this model will be used instead. Leave empty to failover to the next channel.
addChannel.visionFallbackReasoningHint: Reasoning effort used when image requests switch to the vision fallback model.
addChannel.historicalImageTurnLimitHint: Images in historical turns beyond this limit are replaced with [Image] placeholder to avoid unnecessary vision fallback. 0 inherits the global setting; minimum is 3 when set.
addChannel.compactModelPlaceholder: Leave empty to use original model
addChannel.compactModelHint: Upstream model name for local compact (compression/summary), bypassing model mapping. Always used for non-Responses protocol channels; for Responses channels, used as fallback when native compact fails.
addChannel.passbackReasoningContentHint: Convert thinking blocks to reasoning_content when forwarding to Claude-protocol upstreams that require OpenAI-style reasoning_content (e.g. mimo).
addChannel.passbackThinkingBlocksHint: Project real reasoning_content into Claude content[].thinking when forwarding to strict Claude thinking-mode upstreams (e.g. DeepSeek/GLM).
addChannel.customHeadersHint: Add or override HTTP headers sent to the upstream service.
addChannel.rpmHint: Only affects capability tests; higher RPM makes tests finish faster.
addChannel.proxyUrlHint: Supports HTTP/HTTPS/SOCKS5 proxy URLs for accessing upstream services through a proxy.
addChannel.requestTimeoutMsHint: Only applies to non-streaming upstream requests. Leave empty to inherit global REQUEST_TIMEOUT.
addChannel.requestTimeoutMsInvalid: Enter an integer from 1000 to 300000 ms, or leave empty to inherit the global timeout.
addChannel.responseHeaderTimeoutMsHint: Maximum wait for upstream HTTP response headers. Relax only for slow-start channels such as local inference.
addChannel.responseHeaderTimeoutMsInvali: Enter an integer from 1000 to 300000 ms, or leave empty to inherit the global timeout.
addChannel.rateLimitSectionHint: Proactively throttle requests before they reach the upstream to avoid 429 errors (ideal for free / low-quota providers)
addChannel.rateLimitRpmHint: Max requests per minute sent upstream for this channel; excess requests queue. 0 or empty = no limit.
addChannel.rateLimitWindowMinutesHint: Sliding window duration in minutes. Example: RPM=50 + window=5 means "max 50 requests per 5 minutes".
addChannel.rateLimitBurstHint: Token bucket size for short bursts. Defaults to the RPM value when empty.
addChannel.rateLimitMaxConcurrentHint: Max in-flight upstream requests; excess requests queue. 0 or empty = no limit.
addChannel.rateLimitAutoFromHeadersHint: Parse upstream Retry-After / x-ratelimit-* headers and apply dynamic cooldown to further avoid 429.
addChannel.streamTimeoutOverrideHint: Custom value overrides the global stream timeout for this channel.
addChannel.streamTimeoutInheritHint: Disabled means this channel inherits the global stream timeout.
addChannel.routePrefixHint: Access this channel via /{prefix}/v1/messages. Multiple channels can share the same prefix.
addChannel.modelMappingHintChat: Configure model name mapping to redirect the requested model to a target model. Example: redirect "gpt-4o" to "gpt-5.4".
addChannel.modelMappingHintGemini: Configure model name mapping to redirect the requested model to a target model. Example: redirect "gemini-pro" to "gemini-3.5-flash".
addChannel.modelMappingHintResponses: Configure model name mapping to redirect the requested model to a target model. Example: redirect "opus" to "gpt-5.4".
addChannel.modelMappingHintMessages: Configure model name mapping to redirect the requested model to a target model. Example: redirect "opus" to "claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929".
addChannel.sourceModelNameTooLong: Custom model name is too long (max 50 characters)
addChannel.sourceModelInvalidChars: Model name can only contain letters, numbers, hyphens, underscores, dots, slashes, colons, @, and +
addChannel.atLeastOneUrl: Please enter at least one URL
addChannel.fillBaseUrlAndApiKey: Fill in Base URL and at least one API key first
addChannel.allApiKeysModelsFailed: No API key could load the model list. Check the error shown for each API key.

channelCard.tooltipError: Connection error: check the Base URL, network, or API key.

channelCard.noVision: This channel does not support image input

toast.loadFuzzyFailed: Failed to load fuzzy mode status. Refresh and try again.
toast.fuzzyUnknown: Fuzzy mode status is unknown. Refresh first.
toast.loadBillingFailed: Failed to load strip billing header status. Refresh and try again.
toast.billingUnknown: Strip billing header status is unknown. Refresh first.
toast.billingToggleFailed: Failed to toggle strip billing header: {message}
toast.enterAccessKeyContinue: Enter the access key to continue
toast.savedKeyInvalid: The saved access key is no longer valid. Enter it again.
toast.tooManyAttemptsSeconds: Too many attempts. Retry in {seconds} seconds.
toast.tooManyAttempts: Too many attempts. Retry in 5 minutes.
toast.accessKeyInvalidRemaining: Access key verification failed ({remaining} attempts left)
toast.authInvalid: Access key is invalid or expired. Enter it again.
tooltip.loadFailedRefresh: Load failed, please refresh the page
tooltip.billingEnabled: Enabled: automatically strips cch= billing parameters from system.
tooltip.billingDisabled: Disabled: keeps full billing header information.
tooltip.fuzzyEnabled: Fuzzy mode is enabled: errors are handled loosely and all channels are retried.
tooltip.fuzzyDisabled: Fuzzy mode is disabled: upstream responses are passed through precisely.
tooltip.circuitBreakerSettings: Tuning Bench: circuit breaker & stream control
toast.circuitBreakerSaved: Tuning Bench settings saved. Changes take effect immediately.
dialog.circuitBreaker.title: Tuning Bench
dialog.circuitBreaker.description: Fine-tune circuit breaker and stream control parameters. Changes take effect immediately.
dialog.historicalImageTurnLimit.title: Historical Image Turn Limit
dialog.historicalImageTurnLimit.label: Keep images from the last N user turns
dialog.historicalImageTurnLimit.hint: Images in turns beyond this limit are replaced with [Image] placeholder. Always on; minimum 3, default 5.
toast.historicalImageTurnLimitFailed: Failed to save historical image turn limit: {message}
toast.loadHistoricalImageTurnLimitFailed: Failed to load historical image turn limit. Refresh and try again.
update.title: System Update
update.upToDate: You are running the latest version
update.available: A new version is available. Please download from GitHub Releases.
guide.title: Getting Started
guide.welcome.title: Welcome to CCX
guide.welcome.body: CCX is a multi-upstream AI proxy and protocol-conversion gateway. Get started in three steps:
guide.welcome.step1: Pick a protocol tab at the top
guide.welcome.step2: Add a channel and fill in your API key
guide.welcome.step3: Point your client at the gateway and start using it
guide.protocol.title: Switch protocol at the top
guide.protocol.body1: The tabs at the top — Claude / Codex / Gemini, and more — each manage their own set of channels.
guide.protocol.body2: Switching a tab changes both the channel list below and what the Add channel button creates.
guide.protocol.body3: Pick the protocol your client speaks first.
guide.addChannel.title: Add a channel
guide.addChannel.body1: Click the Add channel button at the top left to add an upstream for the current protocol.
guide.addChannel.body2: Just paste a Base URL and API key — CCX detects the rest automatically.
guide.channelList.title: Reading the channel list
guide.channelList.intro: Each row is a channel. The example below shows a normal channel and a paused one:
guide.channelList.clickName: Click the channel name or the key-count badge to open the channel editor.
guide.channelList.clickRow: Click the middle of the row to expand the usage stats chart.
guide.channelList.clickLogs: Click the log button on the right to view that channel’s logs.
guide.channelList.clickResume: Click the resume button (shown on paused or tripped channels) to restore the channel right away.
guide.channelList.drag: Drag the handle on the left to reorder priority and the failover sequence.

addChannel.modelListEmptyHint: No model candidates yet. Fill in Base URL and API key, then focus a model input to load /models automatically.
addChannel.modelCapabilitiesRowsHint: Model redirect targets are added here automatically with built-in presets when available. You can still add passthrough or unmapped models manually.
addChannel.modelCapabilityModelPlacehold: Select or enter an upstream model
addChannel.modelCapabilitiesRowsInvalid: Check model capability rows. Model names are required; token and price values must be non-negative.
